strengths: Clear problem framing: truth maintenance in long-horizon agent memory.
Novel combination of temporal knowledge graphs and self-organizing note graphs.
Strong empirical results on temporal-reasoning slice (+15.3pp over baseline).
Efficiency gains demonstrated (lower token count and latency).
Ablation study isolates the contribution of the linker mechanism.
weaknesses: Overall accuracy (62.0%) is lower than flat baseline (66.0%).
Limited evaluation scale (50-question slice of LongMemEval-S).
F1 score (0.174) is lower than baselines, suggesting retrieval precision/recall trade-off.
required_revisions: Clarify the discrepancy between the abstract's claim of +15.3pp 'over the strongest baseline' and the results table showing flat RAG as strongest on overall accuracy.
Justify the stratified slice selection (50 questions) and provide full benchmark results if available.
Include statistical significance tests for reported improvements.
Explain the drop in KU and overall accuracy; discuss the trade-off between temporal reasoning and general retrieval.
Provide more details on the 'maximum-consistent subgraph' algorithm's optimality guarantee and the assumption Δ ≤ 2.
Clarify the placeholder numbers in the conclusion (e.g., 'overall improvement of -2.0').

strengths: Clear problem framing: truth maintenance for long-horizon agents
Novel method: claim-level memory with truth-maintenance edges
Strong ablation isolating the contribution of the linker
Efficiency analysis shows lower compute than baseline
weaknesses: Overall accuracy on primary benchmark is lower than flat baseline
No statistical significance testing (e.g., confidence intervals)
Limited baseline comparison (only flat RAG and A-Mem)
Small evaluation slice (50 questions, TR n=13)
No analysis of failure modes beyond qualitative example
Missing details on cross-domain evaluation results
required_revisions: Report statistical significance (e.g., confidence intervals) for accuracy differences, especially on the small TR slice (n=13).
Clarify the cross-domain evaluation results on LoCoMo and MemoryAgentBench; currently only mentioned but not reported.
Include a more comprehensive baseline (e.g., a simple time-stamped vector store) to better isolate the contribution of the graph structure vs. temporal metadata.
Add error analysis quantifying common failure modes (e.g., parser mistakes, linker errors) beyond a single qualitative example.
Justify the choice of the 50-question stratified slice and report full benchmark results if available.
Specify the exact version and configuration of the DeepSeek-V3.2 reader for reproducibility.

strengths: Clear problem definition: truth maintenance for long-horizon agents
Novel integration of truth maintenance with graph-based memory
Strong empirical gain on temporal-reasoning slice (+15.3pp)
Ablation study isolates contribution of linker mechanism
weaknesses: Overall accuracy lower than flat baseline (62.0 vs 66.0)
No analysis of failure modes on non-temporal questions
Placeholder numbers for some results (e.g., cross-domain)
No discussion of system robustness to noisy claim extraction

Clarify why overall accuracy drops despite TR gain
